output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Get It Back Fast
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what landlords can legally deduct, and how to demand your deposit back today.
content_outline: Why Your Landlord Is Withholding Your Security Deposit (Common Excuses vs. Legal Reality)
Tenant Rights 101: How Long a Landlord Has to Return Your Deposit and What They Can Deduct
Can Your Landlord Keep Your Deposit? Understanding Normal Wear and Tear vs. Damage
What to Do When Your Landlord Doesn't Return Your Deposit: The Dispute Letter Strategy
Escalating Beyond the Letter: Small Claims Court and Damage Recovery
key_points: State laws set strict deadlines for landlords to return security deposits, typically between 14 and 60 days
Landlords cannot deduct for normal wear and tear—only actual damage beyond reasonable use
A formal security deposit dispute letter is the critical first step to document your claim and satisfy legal notice requirements
Wrongful withholding may entitle tenants to statutory damages, attorney fees, and court costs in many jurisdictions
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Use DepositBack to generate a state-specific security deposit demand letter in minutes and start your recovery today.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlords are wrongfully withholding, deducting from, or refusing to return their security deposit
